title: paspalum jesuiticum
intro: Paspalum jesuiticum, also known as Jesuit's grass or Devil's Grass, is a perennial grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and pain due to its flavonoid and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Paspalum jesuiticum include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. Herbal preparations of Paspalum jesuiticum can be made into teas, infusions, or extracts, and are often used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
